commit.message:
Add parallel subagents and scheduled autonomous agents

Parallel subagents:
- /obsidian-save spawns per-type agents (people, projects, tasks, decisions, ideas)
- /obsidian-health spawns per-category agents (links, duplicates, frontmatter, staleness, orphans)
- /obsidian-recap spawns one agent per daily note for parallel reading
- /obsidian-init spawns agents for dashboard, templates, boards, and note samples simultaneously

Scheduled autonomous agents:
- obsidian-morning: daily 8am — creates daily note, surfaces overdue tasks
- obsidian-nightly: daily 10pm — closes out the day, appends summary
- obsidian-weekly: Fridays 6pm — generates weekly review note
- obsidian-health-check: Sundays 9pm — runs health check, saves report only

README updated with new Features entries and Scheduled Agents section with setup instructions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: AuthGate was redirecting all patient tabs back to dashboard

The inAuthFlow check matched all /patient/* routes, so navigating to
Bookings, Records, Pharmacy, or Profile immediately triggered a redirect
back to the home tab. Now inAuthFlow only matches the splash screen and
login/OTP screens, and adds proper redirect-to-login for expired tokens
on protected routes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: handle Windows Terminal trailing whitespace stripping in paste events (#58)

Windows Terminal strips trailing whitespace from each line and trailing
newlines when forwarding clipboard content via bracketed paste. This
caused _on_paste to treat the paste as external, overwriting the local
clipboard (losing trailing spaces) and resetting the line-copy flag
(breaking VS Code line-paste behavior).

Use a trailing-whitespace-tolerant comparison (rstrip per line after
rstrip on the whole string) so that whitespace-only differences
preserve the local clipboard and line-copy state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Overhaul scripts, add experiment tracking, and document organization

Scripts:
- Replace nemo_sif_exec/shell and vllm_v012_exec/shell with unified
  nemo_sif.sh and vllm_sif.sh (no-args = interactive shell, args = exec)
- Remove vllm patch file binds (patches didn't work)
- Remove old Option A pytorch-devel SIF scripts (build_sif, sif_exec,
  sif_shell, eval_slurm, train_slurm, install_deps, setup_and_eval)
- Update all references across SLURM scripts, Python files, and docs

Eval:
- Switch evaluation to vLLM offline inference (run_eval_vllm.py)
- Remove old run_eval.py (transformers-based, superseded)
- Auto-name preds files with SLURM_JOB_ID or timestamp prefix

Experiment tracking:
- Add --experiment flag to train.py, train_grpo.py, run_eval_vllm.py
- Prefixes all artifact names: nemotron-lora/<exp>_<job_id>/,
  preds/<exp>_<job_id>_*.json
- Auto-writes JSON manifest to experiments/<exp>/ on completion
  (adapter path, accuracy scores, config snapshot)
- Add experiments/ to .gitignore

Docs:
- Add CLAUDE.md with full architecture, common commands, experiment
  best practices, eval config reference, and project layout
- Add EXPERIMENTS.md and RESULTS.md (experiment log + results table)
- Add GRPO trainer (train_grpo.py), prompt strategy hints (prompts.py)
- Add configs: base.yaml updates, eval_subset.json, eval_subset_by_qtype.json

commit.message:
ci: add release-please, dependabot, and PR title validation (#626)

* ci: add release-please, dependabot, and PR title validation

Replace manual tag-based release workflow with release-please for
automated changelog generation and version bumps via conventional
commits. Add Dependabot for weekly dependency scanning with auto-merge
for minor/patch updates. Add PR title validation to enforce conventional
commit format.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove Renovate in favor of Dependabot

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address blueprint validation gaps

- pr-title.yaml: add release-please branch trigger so the check passes
  on release-please PRs (required if "PR Title" is a required check)
- release-please-manifest: seed at 6.2.0rc0 (latest tag, not 6.1.0)
- release.yaml: fix shell precedence bug in update-citation-date job
- Remove dead scripts/extract_release_notes.py (no longer used)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* revert release please base version

* fix: add tag-triggered prerelease support, switch to simple release type

- Add `v*.*.*` tag trigger so manually pushed tags run the full
  build/publish pipeline and create a GitHub prerelease
- Introduce `release-info` hub job that unifies release-please and
  tag-push paths for downstream jobs
- Switch release-type from "python" to "simple" (setuptools_scm derives
  version from git tags, no static version files to bump)
- Skip docs deploy and citation update for prereleases
- pr-title.yaml: drop redundant `synchronize` trigger, add `revert`
  and `style` types

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: split release into release.yaml + publish.yaml

Adopt the blueprint pattern: release.yaml handles release-please on
main, publish.yaml is a reusable workflow with two triggers:
- workflow_call: called by release-please for stable releases
- push tags v*: manual tag push for prereleases

The github-release job only runs on tag push (release-please creates
its own release). Prerelease detection uses PEP 440 patterns
(rc/alpha/beta) instead of semver hyphens.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: address review feedback on publish.yaml

- Start verify-pypi retry at 30s instead of 10s (PyPI propagation)
- Add clarifying comment on github-release condition

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add multi-database support (SQLite, MySQL, PostgreSQL)

Introduces provider-aware database initialisation so Authme can run
against PostgreSQL (production default), MySQL 8+/MariaDB, or SQLite
(development / CI) without code changes — only DATABASE_URL needs to
change.

Changes:
- prisma/schema.sqlite.prisma: full SQLite-compatible schema variant
  (Json→String, Bytes→String/base64, BigInt→Int, arrays→JSON text,
   @db.VarChar annotations removed, enums→String)
- prisma/schema.mysql.prisma: full MySQL-compatible schema variant
  (Json/Bytes/BigInt kept as native MySQL types, String[] columns
   mapped to Json, @db.VarChar(191) index-prefix annotations added
   throughout to respect the 767-byte utf8mb4 index limit)
- prisma/schema.prisma: added provider-compatibility reference table
  and links to variant schemas in the header comment block
- src/database/database-provider.ts: detectProvider() pure function
  that derives the active DatabaseProvider enum from DATABASE_URL
- src/database/database.service.ts: DatabaseService extends PrismaClient,
  detects provider at startup, lazy-loads @prisma/adapter-pg for
  PostgreSQL only, exposes .isPostgres / .isMysql / .isSqlite helpers
- src/database/database.module.ts: @Global NestJS module that provides
  and exports DatabaseService
- src/database/database-provider.spec.ts: 18 unit tests covering all
  three providers, error paths, and enum values (all passing)
- scripts/use-sqlite.sh: backs up active schema and installs the SQLite
  variant in place, prints next-step instructions
- scripts/use-mysql.sh: same flow for the MySQL variant
- docker-compose.dev.yml: added commented-out MySQL service block with
  utf8mb4 config, SQLite usage note, DATABASE_URL examples for all
  three providers
- .env.example: documented all three DATABASE_URL options with links
  to the switch scripts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: Authme
repository.description: Open-source Identity and Access Management Server (Keycloak alternative)

commit.message:
feat(plugins): implement plugin & extension system (Feature #26)

Adds a full plugin infrastructure to AuthMe allowing third-party code
to extend auth providers, enrich tokens, listen to events, and provide
custom themes — all without modifying core source.

Changes:
- src/plugins/ — new module with PluginRegistry, PluginLoaderService,
  PluginManagerService, PluginsController and full TypeScript interfaces
  (AuthMePlugin, EventListenerPlugin, TokenEnrichmentPlugin,
  AuthProviderPlugin, ThemePlugin, PluginContext)
- plugins/example-event-logger/ — example EventListenerPlugin that logs
  all auth events; subscribes to wildcard '*'
- plugins/example-token-enrichment/ — example TokenEnrichmentPlugin that
  adds app_version, platform and enriched_at claims to access tokens
- prisma/schema.prisma — new InstalledPlugin model (installed_plugins table)
- prisma/migrations/20260324400000_add_plugin_system/ — SQL migration
- src/app.module.ts — registers PluginsModule globally
- src/events/events.service.ts — dispatches login events to
  EventListenerPlugin instances (non-blocking, isolated)
- src/auth/auth.service.ts — calls enrichToken() on TokenEnrichmentPlugin
  instances before signing access tokens (non-blocking, isolated)
- src/prisma/prisma.mock.ts — adds installedPlugin, customAttribute,
  userAttribute mock tables
- 55 unit tests covering registry, loader and manager (all passing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(e2e): expand E2E test suite with 7 new test suites

Add comprehensive E2E test coverage across authentication, security, and
delivery subsystems. Also fix a pre-existing bug in setup.ts where
clientSecretHash and passwordHash were defined after use (temporal dead zone).

New test suites:
- test/oauth-grant-types.e2e-spec.ts: Authorization Code+PKCE(S256), Client
  Credentials, Device Authorization (full flow), and Refresh Token grant
  with rotation and scope preservation
- test/oidc-flows.e2e-spec.ts: Discovery document validation, JWKS endpoint,
  JWT access/id_token claims (sub, iss, aud, exp, iat), UserInfo endpoint
- test/mfa-flows.e2e-spec.ts: TOTP enrollment, activation via service layer,
  recovery code generation and single-use enforcement, MFA disable flow
- test/brute-force.e2e-spec.ts: Account lockout after N failures, lockout
  duration/auto-unlock, admin unlock endpoint, permanent lockout threshold
- test/password-policy.e2e-spec.ts: Minimum length, uppercase/lowercase/digit/
  special character requirements, password history (prevent reuse)
- test/webhook-delivery.e2e-spec.ts: Webhook CRUD, event type filtering,
  test endpoint + delivery log, HMAC-SHA256 signature verification
- test/rate-limiting.e2e-spec.ts: Rate limit headers, 429 on limit exceeded,
  Retry-After header, window reset, independent per-client buckets

Bug fix in test/setup.ts:
- Move argon2 hash computation before prisma.realm.create to prevent
  ReferenceError from const temporal dead zone
- Add 'refresh_token' to the default client grant types

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

results: crypto29 daily autoresearch sweep on local 3090 Ti

Best: robust_reg_tp005_sds02 (holdout=-231.5)
- wd=0.05, obs_norm, 8bps slip, tp=0.005, smooth_downside=0.2
- Smooth downside penalty is the key differentiator

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 3 critical data integrity issues from staff engineer audit

- CRIT-1: Atomic enqueue via Redis Lua script to prevent double-queue race
- CRIT-2: Remove FireAndForget from correctness-critical Redis writes
- CRIT-3: Scoped DequeuePlayer routing to eliminate broadcast fan-out

Also adds in-memory duplicate guard in PoolActor, zombie cleanup in
GetQueueAsync, recovery duplicate protection, and queue state tracking
in WebSocketHandler for targeted dequeue routing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(webauthn): implement WebAuthn/FIDO2 passwordless authentication

Adds full passkey support per GitHub issue #259:

- Prisma model: WebAuthnCredential (credential_id, public_key, counter,
  transports, device_type, backed_up, friendly_name) with Realm and User
  relations
- Realm model: webAuthnEnabled, webAuthnRpName, webAuthnRpId fields
- WebAuthnService: generateRegistrationOptions, verifyRegistration,
  generateAuthenticationOptions, verifyAuthentication, getUserCredentials,
  removeCredential, hasCredentials — uses PendingAction table for
  challenge storage (5-min TTL, single-use)
- WebAuthnController: register/verify and authenticate/verify endpoints
  under /realms/:realm/webauthn/, plus account credential management
  endpoints at /realms/:realm/account/webauthn/credentials
- Login integration: passkey login button on login page (hidden when
  webAuthnEnabled is false), uses resident-key / usernameless flow with
  JS fetch + WebAuthn browser API
- Account page: passkey management section (register new passkey, list
  existing with device info, remove), gated on webAuthnEnabled
- Well-known endpoint: advertises webauthn_registration_endpoint and
  webauthn_authentication_endpoint in OIDC discovery document
- Unit tests: 28 tests covering all WebAuthnService methods including
  ForbiddenException for disabled realms, challenge expiry, duplicate
  credential detection, counter updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(session-13): break room, drag-reorder, merge tasks, pebble color picker, system color consistency

Features:
- Break room: floating pause button (bottom-left) + full-screen overlay with breathing
  circle, random tip, Escape/outside-click to close. Portal-based, accessible from all
  pages except /focus.
- Drag-to-reorder tasks within groups via @dnd-kit. 8px activation distance separates
  tap-to-expand from drag. DragHandle (6-dot grid) appears on row hover.
- Task merge from chat: TASK MERGING RULE in system prompt, merge_tasks ###ACTIONS type,
  mergeTasks Redux reducer, MergePreviewCard in Home.jsx.
- Pebble identity color picker: clicking TopNav avatar opens sage/sky/lilac/amber swatch
  popover. Saves to Redux + Cosmos via savePreferences({ pebble_color }).
- Tasks expand in place: unified TaskRow, expandedTaskId state, first task auto-expands.
- Hide completed tasks: text toggle, hidden by default.

System color consistency:
- All rgba(42,122,144,...) replaced with var(--color-pebble) / var(--color-pebble-soft)
  throughout: nav hover, user bubble, user avatar, new chat button, break room, settings,
  tasks heading, documents glow.
- Nav hover now matches active state (both use --color-pebble).
- Page heading dots standardized: "your tasks●", "settings●", "taking a little break●"

Loading dots:
- All 3-dot bounce animations unified: #50946A (green) → #E0A060 (orange) → #9A88B4 (lilac)
  Hardcoded, never changes with pebble color selection.

Backend:
- RESOURCE SUGGESTION RULE added to _BLOCK_12_BASE in chat_service.py
- Block 8 now passes 800-char content excerpt per document (was metadata-only summary)
- Upload handler stores 2000 chars instead of 500

commit.message:
feat(api): add agent permission management endpoints

Add 6 new admin API endpoints for managing agent permissions:
- GET /api/admin/agents - List all agents with owner and shared users
- GET /api/admin/agents/:agentId/permissions - View single agent's permissions
- GET /api/admin/users/:userId/permissions - View single user's permissions
- POST /api/admin/agent-permissions - Batch grant permissions
- DELETE /api/admin/agent-permissions - Batch revoke permissions
- POST /api/admin/agent-permissions/transfer-owner - Transfer ownership

Also add agentModel.findAll() method to support listing all agents.

feat(task-creation): replace silent decompose with editable preview card

Backend:
- New TaskSuggestion + TaskSuggestionRequest models
- ai_service.suggest_task(): reads last 10 messages, suggests ONE focused task
  with tight system prompt enforcing human titles like "Study for calc exam"
  not "Academic preparation for upcoming examination"
- POST /api/suggest-task endpoint with Content Safety output screening
- Returns needs_clarification:true + question if conversation lacks enough info

Frontend:
- TaskPreviewCard: inline chat bubble with editable title (DM Serif Display),
  description textarea, duration selector, due date chip; pebble-color left border;
  "looks good" saves + navigates, "not quite" dismisses and focuses input
- handleTaskNavigate → calls suggestTask with last 10 conversation messages
  instead of blindly decomposing the AI message text
- PulseDot shown while suggestion loads ("thinking of the right task...")
- Pebble asks a clarification question when conversation has insufficient info
- On confirm: Redux addGroup (pre-generated ID), fire-and-forget Cosmos save,
  navigate('/tasks', {state:{highlightGroupId}})
- store.js addGroup accepts optional id param (backward compatible)
- Tasks.jsx: ocean sage pulse glow + auto-expand on highlightGroupId from nav state

feat(adaptive-learning): wire memory + pattern write paths, strengthen accessibility in AI

- db.py: add upsert_learned_patterns() — was missing, only get existed
- chat_service.py: _MEMORY_TRIGGERS regex detects "remember that...", "don't forget", etc.
- _extract_memory_from_message(): extracts + deduplicates user memories → Cosmos user_memory
- _detect_new_patterns(): writes time-of-day, stress, and task-completion patterns → Cosmos learned_patterns
- _persist_conversation(): now calls both write paths fire-and-forget after every chat turn
- _fmt_block_2(): adds bionic_reading + accessibility font signals + timer_length to GPT context
- ACCESSIBILITY RULE added to Block 2: reading level and comm style are stated cognitive needs, not suggestions

feat(session-13): walkthrough tour, sessions dropdown, UI polish

Walkthrough overlay:
- Navigates to each tab (documents, tasks, focus, settings) so user sees real UI
- Moved from Home.jsx to App.jsx for persistent mount across all routes (fixes infinite loop bug)
- Single React tree position prevents remount when entering/leaving /focus
- sessionStorage step persistence as safety net against accidental remounts
- pageStep flag: light 18% dim (no blur) so page content is visible behind card
- page step cards centered via flex-end + padding (fully rounded, no clipping)
- 0.9s initial delay on first appearance for smoother entrance
- Spotlight glow: separated opacity vs boxShadow transitions (fixes flicker bug)
- Card centered horizontally over spotlight target element

Sessions dropdown:
- Redesigned "pick up where you left off" pill button with chevron
- Categorised sections: chats (teal), tasks (sky), documents (amber)
- Fetches recent documents from API on open
- Pulls task groups from Redux
- Empty state + "ask pebble instead" footer

Home chat:
- "new chat" button: larger (36px), teal-tinted background, visible + icon

Onboarding:
- Single "Let's begin" pill (removed 5-button layout)
- Back button on all question stages
- Continue button on theme stage (replaces auto-advance)
- Slower/smoother Let's begin entrance (1.9s delay, 1.1s duration)
- Pebble lowercase voice throughout

FocusMode:
- All root backgrounds set to transparent so living gradient shows through

Global CSS:
- Living animated background gradient on body::before (22s breathe cycle)
- Night mode gradient override (cool ocean orbs)
- --color-pebble lightened: #5A8A80 → #6FA99E
- btn-primary shimmer slowed 4s → 10s, hover darkens via brightness filter
- Night mode btn-primary: flipped gradient direction (less glaring)
- Walkthrough card: bg-card → bg-primary for brightness

- Added SimplePaint to capture-windows.ps1

- getting-started.md: buttons use D2D, borders use flat GDI
